title: Database connection pool exhausted under traffic spike
error_message: HikariPool-1 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms
stack_trace:
com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool$PoolInitializationException
  at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.checkFailFast
  at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.<init>
  at com.zaxxer.hikari.HikariDataSource.<init>
  at com.app.db.DataSourceConfig.dataSource
root_cause: Connection pool size was set to 10, but traffic spike caused 50+ concurrent requests. Pool exhausted under load, new requests blocked waiting for a connection that never freed.
fix: Increased pool size to 50. Added connection timeout fallback. Implemented circuit breaker pattern. Added pool utilization metric to dashboards.

title: Memory leak in image processing worker
error_message: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
stack_trace:
java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
  at java.awt.image.DataBufferByte.<init>
  at com.app.ImageProcessor.resize
  at com.app.MediaWorker.processJob
root_cause: BufferedImage objects were not being released after processing. Accumulation over 6 hours caused heap exhaustion. Worker processes images in tight loop without explicit GC hints.
fix: Added explicit dispose() calls on BufferedImage. Implemented try-with-resources for image streams. Increased heap to 4GB as temporary fix. Added heap usage alerting at 80%.

title: Redis cache stampede on cold start
error_message: MISSES spike: 99.8% cache miss rate detected. DB CPU at 100%
stack_trace:
RedisException: Connection refused
  at redis.clients.jedis.Connection.connect
  at redis.clients.jedis.BinaryJedis.connect
  at com.app.cache.RedisCache.get
root_cause: After Redis restart, all cache keys expired simultaneously. Thundering herd hit the DB directly with 10,000 req/s.
fix: Implemented cache warming script on startup. Added jitter to TTL values. Introduced probabilistic early expiration to prevent simultaneous expiry.

title: Kubernetes pod OOMKilled loop in analytics worker
error_message: OOMKilled: exit code 137. Pod restarted 14 times in 30 minutes.
stack_trace:
Container exceeded memory limit of 512Mi. Last known state: OOMKilled.
kubectl describe pod analytics-worker-7d4f9b: OOMKilled
root_cause: A new analytics query was loading entire user activity table into memory. No pagination was implemented. Table had grown to 80M rows since query was last tested.
fix: Refactored query to use streaming cursor with batch size of 1000. Increased pod memory limit to 1Gi as interim fix. Added memory profiling to CI pipeline.

title: Deadlock in order processing transactions
error_message: SQLState: 40001 - Deadlock found when trying to get lock; try restarting transaction
stack_trace:
com.mysql.jdbc.exceptions.jdbc4.MySQLTransactionRollbackException: Deadlock found
  at sun.reflect.NativeConstructorAccessorImpl.newInstance0
  at com.app.order.OrderRepository.updateStock
  at com.app.order.OrderService.placeOrder
root_cause: Two transactions acquiring locks on order and inventory tables in opposite order. Race condition under high order volume (Black Friday weekend).
fix: Standardized lock acquisition order across all transactions. Added retry logic with exponential backoff for deadlock errors. Reduced transaction scope.

title: API rate limit breach causing 429 cascade on payment batch
error_message: HTTP 429 Too Many Requests from Stripe API. Retry-After: 60
stack_trace:
stripe.error.RateLimitError: Request rate limit reached for requests/min
  at stripe.api_requestor.APIRequestor.interpret_response
  at stripe.api_requestor.APIRequestor.request
root_cause: Batch job sending 1000 payment status checks in parallel. Stripe limit is 100 req/s. No rate limiting on our side. Retry logic made things worse by doubling request volume.
fix: Implemented token bucket rate limiter. Switched to Stripe webhooks instead of polling. Added queue with 50 req/s limit. Retry now uses exponential backoff with jitter.

title: SSL certificate expiry causing auth failures
error_message: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED] certificate has expired (_ssl.c:1129)
stack_trace:
ssl.SSLCertVerificationError: [SSL: CERTIFICATE_VERIFY_FAILED]
  at urllib3.connectionpool.HTTPSConnectionPool._new_conn
  at urllib3.connectionpool.HTTPSConnectionPool._make_request
root_cause: SSL cert for internal auth service expired. Auto-renewal cron job had silently failed 30 days earlier due to permission change on cert directory.
fix: Renewed certificate manually. Fixed cron job (was running as wrong user). Added 30-day-before-expiry alerting via Datadog.

title: Elasticsearch index shard allocation failure
error_message: cluster_block_exception: blocked by: [FORBIDDEN/12/index read-only / allow delete (api)]
stack_trace:
elasticsearch.exceptions.RequestError: RequestError(403, 'cluster_block_exception')
  at elasticsearch.transport.Transport.perform_request
  at elasticsearch.client.Elasticsearch.index
root_cause: Disk usage exceeded 85% threshold. Elasticsearch auto-applied read-only mode to protect data integrity. Logs index had not been rotated for 3 months.
fix: Freed disk space by deleting old indices. Lowered watermark thresholds. Added disk usage alerting at 70%. Enabled ILM policy for automatic log rotation.

title: gRPC deadline exceeded on recommendation engine inference
error_message: StatusCode.DEADLINE_EXCEEDED: Deadline of 2000ms exceeded
stack_trace:
grpc._channel._InactiveRpcError: <_InactiveRpcError of RPC that terminated with:
  status = StatusCode.DEADLINE_EXCEEDED
  details = "Deadline Exceeded"
  debug_error_string = "UNKNOWN:Error received from peer {grpc_message:"Deadline Exceeded"}"
root_cause: ML model inference taking 3-5s under load. Default 2s gRPC deadline was too aggressive. Model had not been optimized post-deployment and was running on CPU-only nodes.
fix: Increased gRPC deadline to 10s. Added async inference queue. Deployed quantized model (4x faster). Added P99 latency alerting at 1.5s.

title: Null pointer exception in user profile aggregation
error_message: NullPointerException at UserProfileAggregator.java:142 - preferences field not initialized
stack_trace:
java.lang.NullPointerException
  at com.app.UserProfileAggregator.aggregate(UserProfileAggregator.java:142)
  at com.app.ProfileService.getProfile(ProfileService.java:78)
root_cause: New users created via OAuth flow did not have preferences object initialized. Legacy code assumed preferences always exists.
fix: Added null check with default preferences initialization. Updated user creation flow to always create preferences object. Added integration test for OAuth user flow.

title: Kafka consumer lag spike — messages not processing
error_message: Consumer group lag: 2,847,293 messages. Processing rate: 0 msg/s
stack_trace:
org.apache.kafka.common.errors.SerializationException: Error deserializing key/value for partition events-0
  at org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.Fetcher.parseRecord
  at org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.Fetcher.fetchRecords
root_cause: A schema change in producer introduced a new required field. Consumers without the updated schema failed to deserialize and halted. All partitions blocked on the bad message.
fix: Rolled back producer schema change. Deployed consumers with updated schema. Implemented schema registry to prevent future mismatches. Added consumer lag alerting at 50k messages.

title: CORS error blocking frontend requests after gateway deployment
error_message: Access to XMLHttpRequest blocked by CORS policy: No 'Access-Control-Allow-Origin' header
stack_trace:
TypeError: Failed to fetch
  at ProductsPage.loadProducts (ProductsPage.js:45)
  at ProductsPage.componentDidMount (ProductsPage.js:22)
root_cause: New API gateway deployment overwrote CORS configuration. Staging environment had different gateway version that preserved config. Config was not stored in IaC.
fix: Restored CORS config on API gateway. Added CORS config to Terraform. Added smoke test that checks CORS headers on deploy.

title: Connection pool timeout in payment service during peak checkout
error_message: HikariPool-2 - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms. Pool stats: total=20, active=20, idle=0, waiting=47
stack_trace:
com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool$PoolInitializationException
  at com.zaxxer.hikari.pool.HikariPool.getConnection
  at com.app.payment.PaymentRepository.save
  at com.app.payment.PaymentService.processCheckout
root_cause: Payment service pool capped at 20 connections. Flash sale caused 10x normal checkout volume. Long-running fraud check queries held connections for 8-12s each, starving the pool.
fix: Increased pool to 40. Moved fraud check to async post-processing. Added connection wait timeout metric. Separated read and write pools.

title: Postgres max_connections exceeded — all services degraded
error_message: FATAL: remaining connection slots are reserved for non-replication superuser connections
stack_trace:
org.postgresql.util.PSQLException: FATAL: remaining connection slots are reserved for non-replication superuser connections
  at org.postgresql.core.v3.ConnectionFactoryImpl.doAuthentication
  at org.postgresql.core.v3.ConnectionFactoryImpl.openConnectionImpl
root_cause: Postgres max_connections set to 100. Connection pools across 8 microservices each set to 20 = 160 potential connections. A deploy restart of all services simultaneously saturated the limit.
fix: Deployed PgBouncer as connection pooler (now handles 2000 app connections → 80 Postgres connections). Set per-service pool max to 10. Added pg_stat_activity alerting.

title: Slow query causing table lock and cascading timeouts
error_message: ERROR 1205 (HY000): Lock wait timeout exceeded; try restarting transaction. Query running for 184s.
stack_trace:
com.mysql.cj.jdbc.exceptions.CommunicationsException: Lock wait timeout exceeded
  at com.app.catalog.ProductRepository.bulkUpdate
  at com.app.catalog.CatalogSyncJob.run
root_cause: Catalog sync job running full-table UPDATE without batching. Locked rows blocked all read queries on the products table. Missing index on updated_at column caused full table scan.
fix: Added index on updated_at. Batched UPDATE into 500-row chunks with commit between batches. Scheduled job to run during low-traffic window. Query timeout set to 30s.

title: Read replica lag causing stale order data
error_message: Replication lag: 47s. Read queries returning data 47 seconds behind primary.
root_cause: Heavy write load on primary during end-of-month invoicing batch. Replica fell behind by 47 seconds. Order status reads routed to replica returned stale 'pending' state for already-completed orders.
fix: Routed order status reads to primary. Added replica lag metric with 5s alert threshold. Deferred invoicing batch to off-peak hours.

title: DB pool leak after ORM version upgrade
error_message: HikariPool connection leak detected. Stack trace of leaked connection: idle timeout 600000ms exceeded
stack_trace:
HikariPool - Connection leak detection triggered for connection com.mysql.cj.jdbc.ConnectionImpl@7f3d1a2b, stack trace follows
  at com.app.notification.NotificationRepo.findPending
root_cause: ORM upgrade changed lazy session closing behavior. Connections no longer auto-closed at end of request. Regression not caught in staging due to lower concurrency.
fix: Explicitly closed sessions in finally block. Added connection leak detection timeout (30s). Rolled back ORM version on notification-service until fix deployed.

title: N+1 query pattern causing DB saturation
error_message: DB CPU: 98%. Slow query log: 14,000 queries/sec against product_variants table.
root_cause: New product listing endpoint fetched base products (1 query) then fetched variants for each product individually (N queries). Page load of 200 products fired 201 queries per request.
fix: Rewrote endpoint to use JOIN with eager loading. Added query count assertion in integration tests (must be < 5 per request). DB CPU dropped to 12% post-deploy.

title: Billing service connection pool exhausted during invoice batch run
error_message: HikariPool-billing - Connection is not available, request timed out after 30000ms. Pool stats: total=15, active=15, idle=0, waiting=47
root_cause: Monthly invoice batch job was scheduled at 18:00, coinciding with peak billing API traffic. Batch held connections for 45–90s per invoice group, starving live API requests. Pool size of 15 was undersized for concurrent batch + API load.
fix: Rescheduled invoice batch to 02:00. Increased pool size to 30. Added a separate pool for batch jobs (pool-batch, max=10) to isolate batch from API traffic. Added pool utilization alert at 70%.

title: Query plan regression after Postgres statistics update
error_message: Slow query: SELECT * FROM orders JOIN line_items ... execution time 43210ms (was 120ms)
stack_trace:
EXPLAIN ANALYZE output: Seq Scan on orders (cost=0.00..892341.00 rows=4500000)
  Filter: (status = 'pending')
  Rows Removed by Filter: 44982103
root_cause: ANALYZE updated table statistics, causing the query planner to switch from index scan to sequential scan on orders table (45M rows). The new plan was 360x slower.
fix: Created partial index on orders(status) WHERE status='pending'. Forced index hint as temporary workaround. Added query regression tests with execution time bounds.

title: gRPC deadline exceeded on inventory availability check
error_message: StatusCode.DEADLINE_EXCEEDED: Deadline of 500ms exceeded on InventoryService/CheckAvailability
stack_trace:
grpc._channel._InactiveRpcError: <_InactiveRpcError of RPC that terminated with:
  status = StatusCode.DEADLINE_EXCEEDED
  details = "Deadline Exceeded"
grpc.StatusCode.DEADLINE_EXCEEDED
  at checkout_service.inventory_client.check_availability
root_cause: Inventory service performing synchronous DB lookup on each call. Under Black Friday load, DB query latency rose to 800ms. 500ms deadline set at checkout service was too tight.
fix: Increased deadline to 2000ms. Added in-memory cache for availability (TTL 5s). Moved inventory checks to async pre-validation step. P99 latency now 120ms.

title: Upstream payment provider timeout causing checkout cascade failure
error_message: ReadTimeoutError: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='api.paymentprovider.com', port=443): Read timed out. (read timeout=5)
stack_trace:
requests.exceptions.ReadTimeout: HTTPSConnectionPool(host='api.paymentprovider.com', port=443): Read timed out.
  at checkout_service.payment.gateway.charge
  at checkout_service.order.processor.finalize_order
root_cause: Payment provider experienced an internal outage. Their API responses took 30-60s. Our 5s timeout triggered, but no fallback or circuit breaker existed. All checkouts failed for 22 minutes.
fix: Implemented circuit breaker (opens after 5 failures in 30s). Added fallback to secondary payment provider. Introduced async payment confirmation flow so checkout no longer blocks on provider response.

title: Retry storm from notification service overloading email API
error_message: HTTP 503 Service Unavailable from SendGrid. Retry queue depth: 284,000. Sending rate: 0/s
stack_trace:
sendgrid.exceptions.UnauthorizedError: 503 Service Unavailable
  at notification_service.email.sender.send
  at notification_service.worker.process_job
  at celery.app.trace.trace_task
root_cause: SendGrid had a 15-minute degradation. Notification service retried immediately with no backoff, multiplying request volume by 50x. Retry queue grew to 284k items and caused worker memory pressure.
fix: Implemented exponential backoff with jitter on retries. Added max retry limit (5 attempts). Capped retry queue depth at 10k with dead-letter queue for overflow. Added SendGrid status page webhook.

title: HTTP 504 gateway timeout on search endpoint under load
error_message: HTTP 504 Gateway Timeout. Upstream timed out (110: Connection timed out) while reading response header from upstream
stack_trace:
nginx error: upstream timed out (110: Connection timed out) while reading response header from upstream
upstream: "http://search-service:8080/api/search?q=..."
root_cause: Search service Elasticsearch queries spiked to 8s under autocomplete load. NGINX upstream timeout was 3s. 40% of search requests were timing out, causing users to see 504 errors.
fix: Increased NGINX upstream timeout to 15s. Added query result caching (TTL 30s) for top 1000 queries. Optimized Elasticsearch query from multi-match to simpler term query for autocomplete path.

title: Circuit breaker stuck open — pricing service returning fallback values
error_message: CircuitBreakerOpenException: pricing-service circuit breaker is OPEN. Fallback: returning cached prices from 6 hours ago.
stack_trace:
io.github.resilience4j.circuitbreaker.CallNotPermittedException: CircuitBreaker 'pricing-service' is OPEN and does not permit further calls
  at com.app.product.PricingClient.getPrice
  at com.app.product.ProductService.enrichWithPricing
root_cause: Pricing service deployment failed silently — pods were running but returning 500s. Circuit breaker opened correctly, but recovery check interval was set to 60 minutes, keeping it open long after pricing service recovered.
fix: Reduced circuit breaker probe interval to 30s. Added deployment health check that validates pricing endpoints return 200. Set up PagerDuty alert when circuit breaker opens for >5 minutes.

title: gRPC channel exhaustion under sustained ML inference load
error_message: StatusCode.UNAVAILABLE: Connection refused. All subchannels are in transient failure state.
stack_trace:
grpc._channel._InactiveRpcError: StatusCode.UNAVAILABLE
  details = "Connection refused"
  at ml_gateway.inference_client.predict
  at ml_gateway.request_handler.handle
root_cause: gRPC channel pool was not configured. All inference requests shared a single channel with max 100 concurrent streams. At 500 RPS, streams backed up, channel entered TRANSIENT_FAILURE, all requests failed.
fix: Configured channel pool (10 channels × 100 streams = 1000 concurrent). Added per-channel health checks. Implemented fallback to synchronous REST endpoint when gRPC unavailable.

title: upstream timeout cascade — recommendation service down
error_message: timeout calling recommendation svc, returning empty list
root_cause: recommendation service slow due to model reload, homepage started returning empty recs, users saw blank recommendations section
fix: added timeout fallback to return cached popular items, set 800ms timeout with fallback

title: Exponential retry loop from shipping webhook causing provider block
error_message: HTTP 429 from FedEx Webhook API. IP blocked after 50,000 requests in 10 minutes.
stack_trace:
requests.exceptions.HTTPError: 429 Client Error: Too Many Requests
  at shipping_service.webhook.notifier.deliver
  at shipping_service.celery_tasks.retry_webhook
root_cause: Shipping webhook delivery task set to retry every 1 second on failure with no max_retries cap. A FedEx API degradation triggered infinite fast retries. One stuck task generated 50k requests in 10 minutes.
fix: Set max_retries=5 with exponential backoff starting at 60s. Added dead-letter queue for exhausted retries. IP unblock requested from FedEx after rate limiter deployed.

title: Service mesh timeout misconfiguration cutting long-running export requests
error_message: upstream request timeout after 15s. Export job was 40% complete.
stack_trace:
Error: context deadline exceeded
  at grpc.(*ClientConn).Invoke
  at export_service.client.RequestExport
istio timeout: 15s global policy applied
root_cause: Istio mesh-wide default timeout of 15s applied to all routes. Long-running CSV export requests take 30-120s for large accounts. Global timeout silently truncated them at 15s with no error surfaced to users.
fix: Added per-route timeout override for export endpoints (300s). Added progress API so frontend polls instead of waiting on HTTP. Documented Istio timeout precedence in runbook.

title: Kafka partition rebalance causing 12-minute consumer processing gap
error_message: Consumer group rebalancing. All consumers paused. Lag growing: +18,000 msg/min during rebalance.
stack_trace:
org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.AbstractCoordinator: Attempt to heartbeat failed since group is rebalancing
  at org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.KafkaConsumer.poll
  at com.app.events.OrderEventConsumer.run
root_cause: Rolling deploy of event-service caused repeated consumer group rebalances. Each new pod joining triggered a full rebalance (12 topics × 20 partitions each). Rebalance took 2-3 minutes, pausing all consumption.
fix: Enabled incremental cooperative rebalancing (CooperativeStickyAssignor). Added consumer startup stagger (30s between pod starts). Lag recovered within 4 minutes post-deploy. Added rebalance duration metric.

title: RabbitMQ queue backlog — notification delivery delayed 4 hours
error_message: Queue depth: 1,847,293. Consumer count: 0. Message TTL expiring. Oldest message: 4h 12m
stack_trace:
pika.exceptions.AMQPConnectionError: [Errno 111] Connection refused
  at pika.adapters.blocking_connection.BlockingConnection.__init__
  at notification_service.mq.consumer.start
root_cause: RabbitMQ memory high-water mark reached (80% of 4GB). RabbitMQ blocked all publishers and paused consumer delivery. Consumers appeared healthy but received nothing. Alert was set on consumer count, not queue depth.
fix: Increased RabbitMQ memory limit. Added queue depth alerting (threshold: 10k). Added lazy queues to reduce memory footprint. Consumers reconnected automatically after memory freed.

title: Celery worker stuck on poison message — task queue fully backed up
error_message: Task report-service.generate_pdf[a3f1b2c4] received SIGKILL after 3600s. Queue: reports. Pending tasks: 4,281.
stack_trace:
celery.exceptions.WorkerLostError: Worker exited prematurely: signal 9 (SIGKILL)
  at celery.worker.consumer.consumer.Consumer.on_task_received
Task ID: a3f1b2c4-...
Report ID: RPT-00891 (malformed template)
root_cause: A malformed report template caused the PDF generator to loop infinitely on one task. Celery worker was single-threaded for that queue. One stuck task blocked all subsequent report generation for 47 minutes.
fix: Added task time limit (300s hard, 240s soft). Moved stuck task to dead-letter queue. Switched report workers to concurrency=4. Added task age alerting when oldest pending task > 60s.

title: SQS queue depth alarm — file processing 6 hours behind
error_message: CloudWatch Alarm: FileProcessingQueue depth > 100,000. Oldest message age: 6h 14m. Expected processing: <5min.
root_cause: S3 bucket policy change blocked GetObject permissions for the file processing worker IAM role. Workers received messages, attempted to fetch files, got AccessDenied, and requeueed silently instead of dead-lettering.
fix: Restored IAM permissions. Added explicit dead-letter queue for S3 access errors. Changed error handling to DLQ on permission errors (non-retryable). Added queue age metric to dashboards.

title: Batch analytics job exhausting memory — OOM killed midway
error_message: MemoryError: Unable to allocate 18.2 GiB for array. Process killed (OOMKilled). Job failed at 67% completion.
stack_trace:
Traceback (most recent call last):
  File "analytics/monthly_aggregation.py", line 312, in run_aggregation
    df = pd.read_sql(query, conn)  # loads full result set
  File "pandas/io/sql.py", line 1748, in read_sql
numpy.core._exceptions._ArrayMemoryError: Unable to allocate 18.2 GiB
root_cause: Monthly analytics aggregation loaded 90 days of raw event data into a pandas DataFrame in a single read_sql call. Dataset had grown 5x since the query was written. Node had 16GB RAM.
fix: Rewrote using chunked iteration (chunksize=50000). Moved aggregation to Spark job on EMR. Added memory profiling to analytics CI. Job now completes in 8 minutes using 2GB peak.

title: Worker process hung on external geocoding API — batch import stalled
error_message: No heartbeat from import-worker-3 for 900s. Batch job stalled at record 14,200/80,000.
root_cause: geocoding api hung, worker waiting with no timeout set
fix: added 10s timeout to geocoding calls, added worker heartbeat monitoring

title: Kafka consumer group lag growing — batch event processor stuck behind
error_message: Consumer group batch-processor lag: 892,441 messages. Topic: user-events. Processing rate degraded: 200 msg/s (normal: 12,000 msg/s)
stack_trace:
org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.CommitFailedException: Commit cannot be completed since the group has already rebalanced
  at org.apache.kafka.clients.consumer.internals.ConsumerCoordinator.sendOffsetCommitRequest
  at com.app.batch.EventBatchProcessor.commitOffsets
root_cause: Batch processor handling took 8s per batch but max.poll.interval.ms was 5s. Kafka treated slow consumers as dead, triggered rebalances, preventing offset commits. Processing slowed to near-zero while fighting constant rebalances.
fix: Increased max.poll.interval.ms to 30s. Reduced max.poll.records to 50 so batch size fits within interval. Consumer lag cleared in 25 minutes post-fix.

title: Memory pressure from unbounded in-memory session store
error_message: Container memory usage: 7.8GB / 8GB limit. GC pause: 12s. Response times: 8000ms (P99). Approaching OOMKill.
stack_trace:
java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: GC overhead limit exceeded
  at java.util.HashMap.resize
  at java.util.HashMap.putVal
  at com.app.session.InMemorySessionStore.put
  at com.app.auth.SessionManager.createSession
root_cause: Session store backed by HashMap with no eviction policy. Sessions kept in memory indefinitely. After 48 hours of traffic, store held 4.2M sessions consuming 7.8GB. GC couldn't keep up.
fix: Replaced in-memory store with Redis (TTL 24h). Added JVM heap alerting at 80%. Existing sessions migrated with 30-minute overlap. Memory usage dropped to 400MB.

title: Batch invoice generation job stalled by database lock contention
error_message: Batch job billing_monthly_invoices paused at 3,421/48,000. Lock wait timeout exceeded on invoices table.
stack_trace:
com.mysql.cj.jdbc.exceptions.CommunicationsException: Lock wait timeout exceeded; try restarting transaction
  at com.app.billing.InvoiceRepository.markGenerated
  at com.app.billing.InvoiceBatchJob.processChunk
root_cause: Monthly invoice batch job and real-time invoice creation API both competing for row locks on the invoices table. Batch job held locks for 2-3s per row due to slow PDF generation in same transaction.
fix: Separated PDF generation from the database transaction. Batch job now marks records, releases lock, then generates PDF. Lock contention eliminated. Batch job completes 8x faster.

title: Worker queue backed up — email sends delayed 3 hours
error_message: email queue depth 45000 workers not keeping up
root_cause: marketing campaign send triggered at 9am caused 45k emails to queue simultaneously, only 3 workers running, each email taking 400ms
fix: auto-scaled workers to 20 for campaign sends, added queue depth dashboard

title: gRPC deadline exceeded — user-facing latency spike during model warmup
error_message: StatusCode.DEADLINE_EXCEEDED on PersonalizationService/GetRecommendations. 68% of requests failing. Deadline: 1000ms.
stack_trace:
grpc._channel._InactiveRpcError: <_InactiveRpcError of RPC that terminated with:
  status = StatusCode.DEADLINE_EXCEEDED
  details = "Deadline Exceeded"
  at personalization.grpc_client.get_recommendations
  at homepage.handler.build_homepage
root_cause: Cold model deployment: new ML model version required 45s warmup after pod start. During warmup, model serving latency was 2000ms+. 1000ms deadline triggered on nearly all requests. Rolling deploy started 3 new pods simultaneously.
fix: Added pod readiness probe that calls model warmup endpoint before accepting traffic. Set minReadySeconds=60 on deployment. Staged rollout: one pod at a time with 2-minute wait.

title: Connection pool contention causing intermittent DB timeouts on reporting
error_message: PoolTimeoutError: QueuePool limit of size 5 overflow 10 reached, connection timed out, timeout 30
stack_trace:
sqlalchemy.exc.TimeoutError: QueuePool limit of size 5 overflow 10 reached, connection timed out, timeout 30
  at sqlalchemy.pool.impl.QueuePool.connect
  at reporting.db.session_factory
  at reporting.views.generate_dashboard
root_cause: Reporting service SQLAlchemy pool sized for light use (5+10). New BI dashboard feature caused 8 concurrent long-running queries per user (each query 15-30s). Two simultaneous dashboard loads exhausted the pool.
fix: Increased pool size to 30. Added read-only replica endpoint for dashboard queries. Set statement_timeout=20s to prevent runaway queries holding connections indefinitely.

title: Upstream geocoding API timeout causing address validation worker to stall
error_message: requests.exceptions.ReadTimeout: HTTPConnectionPool(host='geocode.internal', port=8080): Read timed out after 60s. Worker idle: 900s.
stack_trace:
requests.exceptions.ReadTimeout
  at address_service.geocoding.validate_address
  at address_service.worker.process_validation_queue
Task pending: 12,847 addresses in queue
root_cause: Internal geocoding microservice had a thread deadlock causing 100% of requests to hang indefinitely. Address validation worker had no timeout on HTTP calls. One hung call froze the entire worker thread for 15 minutes.
fix: Added 10s connect/30s read timeout to all geocoding calls. Added health check to geocoding service (deadlock detection). Worker now skips and requeues on timeout. Fixed geocoding deadlock (ThreadPoolExecutor misuse).

title: Memory leak in PDF rendering worker causing weekly OOMKill
error_message: java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space at com.itextpdf.kernel.pdf.PdfWriter. Pod OOMKilled every ~7 days.
stack_trace:
java.lang.OutOfMemoryError: Java heap space
  at com.itextpdf.kernel.pdf.PdfWriter.<init>
  at com.itextpdf.kernel.pdf.PdfDocument.<init>
  at com.app.billing.PdfGenerator.generateInvoice
root_cause: iText PdfDocument objects not closed after PDF generation. Each document leaked ~2MB of native memory off-heap. Over 7 days, 500 invoices/day × 2MB = 3.5GB leaked. Pod limit was 4GB. Weekly restart was masking the issue.
fix: Wrapped PdfDocument in try-with-resources. Added native memory monitoring (not just JVM heap). Pod uptime now exceeds 30 days without OOMKill. Added integration test that generates 1000 PDFs and checks memory growth.

title: API gateway upstream timeout — product search returning 504s
error_message: upstream timed out (110: Connection timed out) while reading upstream, client: 203.0.113.45, upstream: http://search-service/search
stack_trace:
nginx: upstream timed out (110: Connection timed out)
2025/03/12 14:22:11 [error] 29#29: *12847 upstream timed out
root_cause: Search service Elasticsearch query slow due to a large unoptimized aggregation query added in last deploy. API gateway had 5s timeout. Query was taking 12s. 100% of search requests returned 504 for 35 minutes.
fix: Reverted Elasticsearch aggregation query. Increased timeout to 20s as hotfix. Optimized query with terms aggregation instead of script aggregation. Query now runs in 800ms.

title: Batch processing delay — nightly ETL job running 6x longer than expected
error_message: ETL job user_events_daily_rollup: expected completion 02:00 UTC, still running at 06:47 UTC. Downstream reports blocked.
root_cause: A new event type added to user_events table increased daily row count from 50M to 340M. ETL job had no partitioning strategy — full table scan on every run. Runtime scaled linearly with data volume.
fix: Partitioned user_events table by date. ETL now processes only yesterday's partition. Runtime reduced from 6h to 22 minutes. Added job duration SLA alerting (fails if >90 min).
